event: Italian comedy theatrical performance: the love note scene
symbols: stage curtains — the boundary between illusion and reality, the theatrical frame separating performers from audience
masked and costumed figures — the assumed identities and social roles of commedia dell'arte stock characters
harlequin's diamond-patterned costume — the trickster figure, wit and mischief personified
architectural ruins as stage backdrop — the classical past as theatrical setting, nostalgia and artifice combined
description: A theatrical stage scene framed by heavy red curtains, depicting a commedia dell'arte performance. Five costumed figures stand on a raised wooden stage before an elaborate painted backdrop of classical ruins and arches. Center stage, a woman in an ornate golden gown holds hands with a man in pale 18th-century attire. A dark-cloaked figure stands between them. To the right, a harlequin in diamond-patterned costume strikes an animated pose. Another masked figure stands at left.
